source_locators: RFC 9935 section 3, extracted-text lines 174-189: ML-KEM public-key AlgorithmIdentifier OIDs and absent parameters. | RFC 9935 section 4, extracted-text lines 221-240: ML-KEM public-key ASN.1 definitions with PARAMS ARE absent.
justification: Malformed ML-KEM AlgorithmIdentifier breaks PKIX interoperability at issuance and consumption time.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 5, extracted-text lines 277-280: keyEncipherment MUST be the only key usage for certificates with id-alg-ml-kem-*.
justification: ML-KEM certificate keyUsage is an issuance policy requirement and should block misprofiled certificates.

source_locators: FIPS 203 section 8 Table 3, extracted-text lines 2139-2144: ML-KEM encapsulation key sizes 800, 1184, and 1568 bytes. | RFC 9935 section 4, extracted-text lines 244-248: ML-KEM PublicKey OCTET STRING sizes 800, 1184, and 1568 bytes.
justification: Wrong ML-KEM public-key length is a structural defect that indicates malformed SPKI encoding.

source_locators: FIPS 203 section 4, extracted-text lines 1286-1297: ByteDecode12 can produce values that cannot occur from ByteEncode12. | FIPS 203 section 7.2, extracted-text lines 2028-2040: encapsulation-key type and modulus checks using ByteEncode12(ByteDecode12(...)).
justification: Unreduced ML-KEM encoded keys are structurally suspicious and should at least surface operationally until local detection is implemented.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 6, extracted-text lines 329-356: ML-KEM PrivateKey CHOICE seed [0] OCTET STRING size 64 for all parameter sets. | RFC 9935 section 6, extracted-text lines 365-399: seed format and ASN.1 tag parsing guidance.
justification: ML-KEM seed-only private keys must match the RFC container size before import proceeds.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 6, extracted-text lines 335-356: expandedKey sizes 1632, 2400, and 3168 bytes. | FIPS 203 section 8 Table 3, extracted-text lines 2139-2144: decapsulation key sizes 1632, 2400, and 3168 bytes.
justification: ML-KEM expanded private keys must match parameter-set sizes before import proceeds.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 8, extracted-text lines 427-432: recipient should check seed/expandedKey consistency and MUST reject if the check fails.
justification: A both-form ML-KEM private key must be rejected on seed/expanded mismatch during import.

source_locators: FIPS 203 section 7.3, extracted-text lines 2073-2088: decapsulation input hash check before Decaps. | RFC 9935 section 8, extracted-text lines 443-445: expandedKey requires FIPS 203 hash check before use.
justification: Expanded ML-KEM private keys require the FIPS/RFC hash check before use.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 2, extracted-text lines 179-189: ML-DSA OIDs and absent parameters. | RFC 9881 section 4, extracted-text lines 336-350: ML-DSA public-key ASN.1 definitions.
justification: Malformed ML-DSA AlgorithmIdentifier breaks PKIX interoperability at issuance and validation time.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 3, extracted-text lines 246-272: ML-DSA signature AlgorithmIdentifier definitions and absent parameters.
justification: ML-DSA certificate signatureAlgorithm encoding is a PKIX conformance requirement and should block issuance defects.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 5, extracted-text lines 381-396: if keyUsage is present for ML-DSA, at least one signing-related bit is required.
justification: ML-DSA certificates need a permitted signing usage when keyUsage is present.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 5, extracted-text lines 404-413: ML-DSA keyUsage MUST NOT contain key-establishment or encryption bits.
justification: ML-DSA certificates must not advertise encipherment or agreement semantics they cannot safely support.

justification: Wrong ML-DSA public-key length is a structural defect that indicates malformed SPKI encoding.
